text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of working on a large project all at once, teams use iterative development. This means they break the work into short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning meeting where the team selects tasks from a backlog, which is a list of all the work needed. These tasks are described as user stories, written from the customer’s perspective. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and any obstacles. This process helps teams adapt quickly to changes and deliver working results regularly.
